channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: April Dining Events
item.description: Get ready for a month full of great food, fun, and community highlighted by the always popular Late Night Breakfast on April 30th from 9pm- 11pm! To see all the events coming up in April click the link above. We hope to see you at one or all of these events!
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week 4/19-4/26
item.description: Week of 4/19- 4/26: Last Round of Exams! Finish strong with one of our Success Center Review Sessions: -Physics I with SASE: Sunday, 4/19 (5-7pm) at the Success Center -Diff Eq.: Sunday, 4/19 (3-5pm) at the Success Center -Embedded Control: Monday 4/20 (7-9pm) at the Success Center -Cal II: Monday, 4/20 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom -Physics II: Tuesday, 4/21 (7-9pm) in Nason Classroom -Data Mgmt: Wednesday, 4/22 in ECAV (8-10pm) (Championship Classroom)
item.author: Students

item.title: 2026 Campus-Wide Planned Steam and Electrical Maintenance Schedule
item.description: Planned campus-wide steam and electrical maintenance is scheduled for Saturday, May 9 through Friday, May 15, 2026. These outages are necessary to complete critical maintenance, inspections, and testing of central utility and electrical infrastructure.
item.author: Community

item.title: Cognitive Science Colloquium with Dr. Fuat Balci on April 22nd
item.description: Please join the Cognitive Science Department on Wednesday, April 22nd, at 12:00 pm in Carnegie 113 for a presentation titled “The Stochastic Bridge: Diffusion in Timing and Time Awareness” by Dr. Fuat Balcı, Full Professor at the University of Manitoba and an internationally recognized expert in cognitive neuroscience.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar-Friday April 24: Webex Training Session
item.description: Join us on Friday, April 24, from 11am-12pm for a hands-on Webex Training Session, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. This session focuses on the Webex application and its integration with Outlook and other productivity tools, so you can efficiently schedule, launch, and manage meetings directly from the tools you use every day. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Join us at EMPAC for Corpus Festival — Opening day & Community Meal! (RSVP)
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 23, 2026 (all day) - EMPAC presents a weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. Bring your curiosity and your appetite: How do we take part in society by physically showing up? RSVP in advance for the community meal — a free, family-style, long-table supper co-hosted by artist Yanira Castro, with food by Chef Ria Ibrahim, musical offerings by Taína Asili — and more. Installations open…
item.author: Community

item.title: From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life
item.description: Please join ISE on Wednesday, April 22 at 12:15 PM in LOW 3039 for a colloquium featuring Dr. Andrew Trapp’s research on From Model-Optimal to Human-Optimal: Human-Centered Optimization for the Common Purposes of Life! All are welcome!
item.author: Community

item.title: Summer Internship Opportunity in the Design Lab!
item.description: Project: Capstone Team Formation and Project Assignment Tool Work description: This project will develop an AI-powered software tool to automate and optimize the student-to-project assignment process. The intern will build ML/NLP-powered tools to extract student competencies from unstructured data, map them to project requirements, and automatically generate optimized student-project matches based on interests and experience — all supported by the Design Lab team. For more information: https:/…
item.author: Community

item.title: Mental Health Carnival with RPI Neurons
item.description: Date: Sunday, April 19, 2026 12:00 PM - 2:00 PM - Following Circle K’s Out of the Darkness walk, join the RPI Neurons in DCC for a Mental Health Carnival! We’ll have games, activities, snacks, prizes, and raffles. Let’s come together to support mental health, spread awareness, and have some fun!
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - This week’s seminar will be presented by Phillip E. Savage, Distinguished Professor of Chemical Engineering at Penn State. His topic will be “Hydrothermal Reactions of Biopolymers and Synthetic Polymers – Toward a more Circular Economy and Renewable Fuels”.
item.author: Community

item.title: IBM “Road to Practitioner” Quantum Program
item.description: IBM has announced its Road to Practitioner (R2P) program—a multi-week online quantum computing course beginning in May, taught by members of IBM’s quantum team. This is an excellent opportunity to learn about the current state of quantum computing and algorithms in a focused, instructor-led setting. Visit https://foci.rpi.edu/news-and-events for more details and the link to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Volunteers Needed: Late Night Breakfast
item.description: We’re looking for volunteers on April 30th from 8:30 pm - 11 pm to help make Late Night Breakfast a success! Volunteers will assist with greeting students, serving food and helping to keep the good vibes going! If you’re interested in helping out or would like more information please email auxiliaryservices@rpi.edu. We’d love to have you on the team!
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty & Staff Appreciation Lunch - April 22nd, 2026
item.description:
Join us on Wednesday April 22nd for a $6 (plus tax) lunch at Commons or Russell Sage Dining Halls as we get towards the end of another academic school year! We hope you can take a break, connect with colleagues, and enjoy this time together.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
